description: Add a dedicated local-only metadata enrichment step to the Firestore initialization path, ensuring passwords (and any future local-only fields) from localStorage are merged into in-memory project objects before they reach React state.
steps: In src/persistenceService.js: Added enrichProjectWithLocalMetadata(project) function that reads the corresponding localStorage entry and merges local-only fields (password) into the project object.

In src/App.jsx: After `loadedProjects = firestoreProjects;`, applied enrichProjectWithLocalMetadata to each project so that passwords from localStorage are present in the in-memory objects before migration and setProjects().
Verified that the password gate initialization (activeProj.password check) will now see the correct value.
Ran npm run build to verify compilation - passes cleanly.
Produced a code-level review classifying all 13 areas requested by the user.
acceptance_criteria: A reusable enrichProjectWithLocalMetadata function exists in persistenceService.js
Firestore-sourced projects are enriched with local-only metadata before flowing into React state
activeProj.password is correctly populated from localStorage for the password gate
npm run build exits with code 0
Code-level review covers all 13 areas with classification
verification: Run npm run build in /projects/sandbox/ClearMind and confirm exit code 0
findings: The enrichment function uses a LOCAL_ONLY_FIELDS array pattern that is extensible for future local-only fields. All 13 areas classified as CONFIDENT. Two additional edge cases (localStorage manually cleared, multi-tab) classified as NEEDS MANUAL TEST. The architecture is now: Firestore metadata + localStorage-only metadata -> enriched projects -> React state.
